title: trifolium longipes subsp. oreganum
intro: Trifolium longipes subsp. oreganum, also known as Oregon bitter clover, is a subspecies of the Trifolium longipes plant, belonging to the legume family and native to the western United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its bitter properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and saponins. The medicinal properties of Trifolium longipes subsp. oreganum include its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential to act as an antibacterial and anti-inflammatory agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its health benefits, including improved digestion, reduced inflammation, and boosted immune function.
